template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST (DECLARATION OF TRUST)**:
A declaration of trust executed by the Grantor(s), who typically serve as initial Trustee(s). Governed by the Massachusetts Uniform Trust Code (MUTC), M.G.L. c. 203E. Funded during life to avoid probate on the Grantor's death. Pair with a pour-over Will. Target length: 6–14 pages depending on complexity.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `trustType`: `individual_grantor` (default) | `joint_grantor` (typically married couples — one combined trust for both) | `separate_joint` (two separate trusts, jointly funded in some assets).
- `disclaimerTrust`: boolean — when true, include a credit-shelter disclaimer trust provision for the Massachusetts estate tax threshold (currently $2 million effective 2023, per Ch. 50 of the Acts of 2023 amending M.G.L. c. 65C, § 2A).
- `irrevocableAtDeath`: boolean (default true) — trust becomes irrevocable upon the Grantor's death (or the death of the surviving Grantor for joint trusts).
- `specialNeedsSubTrust`: boolean — when true, carve out a supplemental needs trust for a beneficiary receiving means-tested public benefits (MassHealth, SSI), drafted to comply with 42 U.S.C. § 1396p(d)(4).
- `perStirpes`: boolean (default true) — per stirpes distribution to descendants of a deceased beneficiary; when false, specify per capita or other scheme.

**STRUCTURE**:

## DECLARATION OF TRUST — [NAME OF TRUST]

THIS DECLARATION OF TRUST is made this _____ day of ________________, 20___, by [Grantor full legal name] [and Co-Grantor full legal name, for joint trust], of [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts (collectively, the 'Grantor'), who hereby declares that he/she/they hold(s) certain property in trust, upon the terms and conditions set forth herein.

### ARTICLE I — NAME OF TRUST
The name of this Trust shall be the '[GRANTOR SURNAME] REVOCABLE TRUST DATED [DATE]' (the 'Trust').

### ARTICLE II — TRUST PROPERTY
The Grantor hereby transfers to the Trust the property described on Schedule A, attached hereto and incorporated by reference. The Grantor may from time to time add additional property to the Trust by deed, assignment, beneficiary designation, or pour-over will, and all such property, together with the proceeds and reinvestment thereof, shall constitute the 'Trust Estate.'

### ARTICLE III — GRANTOR'S RIGHTS DURING LIFETIME
During the Grantor's lifetime, the Grantor reserves the following rights, exercisable at any time without the consent of any other party:
(a) To revoke or amend this Trust, in whole or in part, by written instrument delivered to the Trustee;
(b) To add or withdraw property;
(c) To change the beneficiaries or their respective shares;
(d) To remove and replace Trustees;
(e) To direct the Trustee as to the administration and investment of the Trust Estate.

While the Grantor is living and has capacity, the Trustee shall pay income and principal to the Grantor (or to persons at the Grantor's direction) as the Grantor requests, and shall accept and follow all Grantor instructions.

### ARTICLE IV — TRUSTEE PROVISIONS
The Grantor shall serve as the initial Trustee. [For joint trusts]: The Grantors shall serve as initial Co-Trustees, either of whom may act alone in all respects.

Upon the death, resignation, or incapacity of the initial Trustee, [Successor Trustee name] shall serve as Successor Trustee. If [Successor] is unable or unwilling, [Alternate Successor Trustee name] shall serve.

Capacity shall be determined by the written certification of two (2) licensed physicians, at least one of whom is the Grantor's primary care physician, stating that the Grantor is unable to manage his/her financial affairs.

No bond or other security shall be required of any Trustee. The Trustee is entitled to reasonable compensation for services rendered, consistent with M.G.L. c. 203E, § 708.

### ARTICLE V — DISPOSITIVE PROVISIONS UPON GRANTOR'S DEATH

**§ 5.1 — Administrative Expenses**
Upon the Grantor's death, the Trustee shall pay from the Trust Estate the Grantor's funeral expenses, the expenses of the last illness, the Grantor's legally enforceable debts, and the costs of administering the Grantor's probate estate and this Trust, to the extent not otherwise provided. The Trustee shall also pay all estate, inheritance, and generation-skipping taxes as a charge against the Trust Estate, without apportionment against any beneficiary or non-probate transfer.

**§ 5.2 — Specific Distributions**
[List any specific dollar amount or tangible property distributions.]

**§ 5.3 — [IF disclaimerTrust AND trustType = joint_grantor] DISCLAIMER TRUST / CREDIT SHELTER**
Upon the death of the first Grantor to die, the surviving Grantor may, by qualified disclaimer consistent with 26 U.S.C. § 2518 and M.G.L. c. 191A, disclaim all or a portion of the deceased Grantor's interest in the Trust Estate. Disclaimed property shall be held in a Disclaimer Trust for the benefit of the surviving Grantor for life, with principal and income distributable for HEMS (health, education, maintenance, support), designed to use the deceased Grantor's Massachusetts estate tax exemption (currently $2,000,000 per M.G.L. c. 65C, § 2A as amended) and federal estate tax exemption. Upon the death of the surviving Grantor, the Disclaimer Trust shall be distributed pursuant to § 5.4.

**§ 5.4 — Residuary Distribution**
Upon the death of the Grantor (or the surviving Grantor in a joint trust), the Trustee shall distribute the remaining Trust Estate to [primary beneficiaries — typically children]. If any beneficiary predeceases the Grantor, that beneficiary's share shall pass [`perStirpes` ? 'per stirpes to his or her then-living descendants' : 'per capita among the surviving beneficiaries'].

**§ 5.5 — Trust for Minor or Young Adult Beneficiaries**
Any share that would otherwise pass outright to a beneficiary under age [twenty-five (25)] shall instead be held in trust for that beneficiary. The Trustee shall distribute income and principal for the beneficiary's health, education, maintenance, and support (HEMS standard, M.G.L. c. 203E, § 814). The trust shall terminate and distribute outright at age [thirty (30) / 1/3 at 25, 1/3 at 30, 1/3 at 35 / other staged distribution].

**§ 5.6 — [IF specialNeedsSubTrust] SPECIAL NEEDS SUB-TRUST**
Any share otherwise distributable to [Beneficiary name], who receives or may receive means-tested public benefits, shall instead be held in a supplemental needs trust. The trustee shall use distributions ONLY to supplement, not supplant, public benefits, consistent with 42 U.S.C. § 1396p(d)(4) and M.G.L. c. 203E, Article VIII. Distributions shall be in kind or directly to providers when possible, not in cash to the beneficiary, to preserve means-tested benefit eligibility.

### ARTICLE VI — TRUSTEE POWERS
In addition to the powers conferred by law, the Trustee shall have all powers enumerated in M.G.L. c. 203E, § 815 (general powers of trustee) and in M.G.L. c. 184B (Trustees' Powers Act), including the power to:
(a) Sell, exchange, and reinvest Trust assets without Court approval;
(b) Invest consistent with the Massachusetts Prudent Investor Act, M.G.L. c. 203C;
(c) Distribute in cash or in kind;
(d) Employ attorneys, accountants, investment advisors, and other professionals, and pay reasonable compensation from the Trust Estate;
(e) Exercise all powers over digital assets consistent with M.G.L. c. 190B, Article VII (MRUFADAA);
(f) Make distributions to or for the benefit of any minor or incapacitated beneficiary directly, to a custodian under the Uniform Transfers to Minors Act (M.G.L. c. 201A), or to a guardian;
(g) Loan, borrow, and pledge Trust assets.

### ARTICLE VII — SPENDTHRIFT PROVISION
No beneficiary's interest shall be subject to voluntary or involuntary transfer, anticipation, assignment, or to the claims of any creditor of such beneficiary. This spendthrift provision is enforceable under M.G.L. c. 203E, § 502.

### ARTICLE VIII — GOVERNING LAW
This Trust shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles. Questions of administration and validity shall be resolved in Massachusetts Probate and Family Court.

### ARTICLE IX — IRREVOCABILITY AT DEATH
Upon the death of the Grantor (or the surviving Grantor in a joint trust), this Trust shall become irrevocable, and no person shall have the power to amend or revoke it, except as the terms of this Trust expressly provide.

### ARTICLE X — DEFINITIONS AND CONSTRUCTION
[Define 'descendants,' 'per stirpes,' 'HEMS,' 'Code' (Internal Revenue Code), and 'MUTC' (M.G.L. c. 203E). Include survivorship language: a beneficiary must survive the Grantor by thirty (30) days, consistent with M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-702.]

### SIGNATURE AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Grantor has executed this Declaration of Trust on the date first written above.

_______________________________
[Grantor full legal name], Grantor and initial Trustee

[For joint trust:]
_______________________________
[Co-Grantor full legal name], Grantor and initial Trustee

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS
[COUNTY] COUNTY, SS.

On this _____ day of ________________, 20___, before me, the undersigned notary public, personally appeared [Grantor (and Co-Grantor)], proved to me through satisfactory evidence of identification, which was [form of ID], to be the person(s) whose name(s) is/are signed on the preceding or attached document and acknowledged to me that he/she/they signed it voluntarily for its stated purpose.

_______________________________
Notary Public
My commission expires: ____________

### SCHEDULE A — INITIAL TRUST PROPERTY
[List initial assets: bank accounts, real estate with legal description, investment accounts, business interests. Fund the Trust by retitling each listed asset into the name of the Trustee.]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 203E` (MUTC) with specific sections `§ 502` (spendthrift), `§ 708` (compensation), `§ 814` (HEMS), `§ 815` (general powers). Cite `M.G.L. c. 190B` (MUPC) for integration with pour-over will. Cite `M.G.L. c. 65C, § 2A` for MA estate tax; confirm the $2M threshold effective October 2023 per Ch. 50 of the Acts of 2023.
- Cite federal authority exactly: `26 U.S.C. § 2518` (qualified disclaimer), `42 U.S.C. § 1396p(d)(4)` (special needs trust safe harbor).
- Notarization of the Grantor's signature is REQUIRED for practical purposes (recording real estate deeds into the Trust requires a notarized instrument). MA does NOT require witness signatures for a trust (that is a will requirement).
- Funding is essential — an unfunded RLT fails to avoid probate. Schedule A should list actual assets, and the client must retitle each.
- When `trustType` = `joint_grantor` and `disclaimerTrust` is true, verify the MA estate tax threshold at execution — the exemption has changed recently (from $1M to $2M effective October 2023).
- When `specialNeedsSubTrust` is true, the trust provisions MUST satisfy 42 U.S.C. § 1396p(d)(4)(A) (first-party / OBRA '93) or (d)(4)(C) (pooled), depending on the funding source.
- Pair this Trust with a Pour-Over Will — note this in the execution folder.
- Target length: 6–14 pages depending on complexity.
hints: Massachusetts Revocable Living Trust under MUTC (M.G.L. c. 203E). Self-declaratory trust — Grantor as initial Trustee. Retains full revocability and amendability during life; becomes irrevocable at death. Pair with pour-over will. Cite § 502 (spendthrift), § 708 (compensation), § 814 (HEMS), § 815 (general powers). MA estate tax threshold $2M effective October 2023 (M.G.L. c. 65C § 2A as amended). Disclaimer trust per 26 USC § 2518 for joint grantors. Special needs sub-trust per 42 USC § 1396p(d)(4)(A). MRUFADAA digital-asset authorization. Notarization required; no witness requirement for trusts. Funding via retitling is CRITICAL — Schedule A must list assets actually moved.
